judgment.summary: Profitable and growing company with a reasonable forward P/E, but burdened by significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 25.5%; next earnings date is 2026-06-23.
positives: Earnings growth 17.3% YoY — strong bottom-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise 25.5% — significant beat on earnings.
Forward P/E 17.17x — attractive relative to trailing P/E and growth estimates.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target price of $401.77.
Dividend yield 1.5% with a 30.6% payout ratio — sustainable dividend.
negatives: Total debt $42.02B vs $8.22B cash — significant net debt.
Operating margin 6.9% and Profit margin 4.9% — margins are relatively thin.
Analyst growth estimate for current quarter is negative (-3.0%).

news.paragraph: FedEx recently received positive commentary from both its own reporting of strong Q1 execution and from a prominent financial commentator. The company beat EPS estimates by a significant margin last quarter, and analysts are projecting solid growth for the current fiscal year. With a consensus 'buy' rating and a mean target price suggesting upside, the news flow is constructive.

headlines.title: FedEx (FDX) Gained From Strong Execution in Q1
Jim Cramer Couldn’t Stop Praising FedEx (FDX)
